speaker: CYMBELINE
text: Thus far, and so farewell.

speaker: LUCIUS
text: Thanks, royal sir.

speaker: LUCIUS
text: My emperor hath wrote I must from hence,

speaker: LUCIUS
text: And am right sorry that I must report you

speaker: LUCIUS
text: My master’s enemy.

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: Our subjects, sir,

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: Will not endure his yoke, and for ourself

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: To show less sovereignty than they must needs

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: Appear unkinglike.

speaker: LUCIUS
text: So, sir. I desire of you

speaker: LUCIUS
text: A conduct overland to Milford Haven.—

speaker: LUCIUS
text: Madam, all joy befall your Grace—and you.

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: My lords, you are appointed for that office.

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: The due of honor in no point omit.—

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: So, farewell, noble Lucius.

speaker: LUCIUS
text: Your hand, my lord.

speaker: CLOTEN
text: Receive it friendly, but from this time forth

speaker: CLOTEN
text: I wear it as your enemy.

speaker: LUCIUS
text: Sir, the event

speaker: LUCIUS
text: Is yet to name the winner. Fare you well.

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: Leave not the worthy Lucius, good my lords,

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: Till he have crossed the Severn. Happiness!

speaker: QUEEN
text: He goes hence frowning, but it honors us

speaker: QUEEN
text: That we have given him cause.

speaker: CLOTEN
text: ’Tis all the better.

speaker: CLOTEN
text: Your valiant Britons have their wishes in it.

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: Lucius hath wrote already to the Emperor

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: How it goes here. It fits us therefore ripely

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: Our chariots and our horsemen be in readiness.

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: The powers that he already hath in Gallia

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: Will soon be drawn to head, from whence he moves

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: His war for Britain.

speaker: QUEEN
text: ’Tis not sleepy business,

speaker: QUEEN
text: But must be looked to speedily and strongly.

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: Our expectation that it would be thus

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: Hath made us forward. But, my gentle queen,

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: Where is our daughter? She hath not appeared

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: Before the Roman, nor to us hath tendered

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: The duty of the day. She looks us like

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: A thing more made of malice than of duty.

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: We have noted it.—Call her before us, for

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: We have been too slight in sufferance.

speaker: QUEEN
text: Royal sir,

speaker: QUEEN
text: Since the exile of Posthumus, most retired

speaker: QUEEN
text: Hath her life been, the cure whereof, my lord,

speaker: QUEEN
text: ’Tis time must do. Beseech your Majesty,

speaker: QUEEN
text: Forbear sharp speeches to her. She’s a lady

speaker: QUEEN
text: So tender of rebukes that words are strokes

speaker: QUEEN
text: And strokes death to her.

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: Where is she, sir? How

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: Can her contempt be answered?

speaker: ATTENDANT
text: Please you, sir,

speaker: ATTENDANT
text: Her chambers are all locked, and there’s no answer

speaker: ATTENDANT
text: That will be given to th’ loud’st noise we make.

speaker: QUEEN
text: My lord, when last I went to visit her,

speaker: QUEEN
text: She prayed me to excuse her keeping close;

speaker: QUEEN
text: Whereto constrained by her infirmity,

speaker: QUEEN
text: She should that duty leave unpaid to you

speaker: QUEEN
text: Which daily she was bound to proffer. This

speaker: QUEEN
text: She wished me to make known, but our great court

speaker: QUEEN
text: Made me to blame in memory.

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: Her doors locked?

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: Not seen of late? Grant, heavens, that which I

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: Fear prove false!

speaker: QUEEN
text: Son, I say, follow the King.

speaker: CLOTEN
text: That man of hers, Pisanio, her old servant

speaker: CLOTEN
text: I have not seen these two days.

speaker: QUEEN
text: Go, look after.

speaker: QUEEN
text:
Aside
                .
                 
              Pisanio, thou that stand’st so for Posthumus—

speaker: QUEEN
text: He hath a drug of mine. I pray his absence

speaker: QUEEN
text: Proceed by swallowing that, for he believes

speaker: QUEEN
text: It is a thing most precious. But for her,

speaker: QUEEN
text: Where is she gone? Haply despair hath seized her,

speaker: QUEEN
text: Or, winged with fervor of her love, she’s flown

speaker: QUEEN
text: To her desired Posthumus. Gone she is

speaker: QUEEN
text: To death or to dishonor, and my end

speaker: QUEEN
text: Can make good use of either. She being down,

speaker: QUEEN
text: I have the placing of the British crown.

speaker: QUEEN
text: How now, my son?

speaker: CLOTEN
text: ’Tis certain she is fled.

speaker: CLOTEN
text: Go in and cheer the King. He rages; none

speaker: CLOTEN
text: Dare come about him.

speaker: QUEEN
text: All the better. May

speaker: QUEEN
text: This night forestall him of the coming day!

speaker: CLOTEN
text: I love and hate her, for she’s fair and royal,

speaker: CLOTEN
text: And that she hath all courtly parts more exquisite

speaker: CLOTEN
text: Than lady, ladies, woman. From every one

speaker: CLOTEN
text: The best she hath, and she, of all compounded,

speaker: CLOTEN
text: Outsells them all. I love her therefore, but

speaker: CLOTEN
text: Disdaining me and throwing favors on

speaker: CLOTEN
text: The low Posthumus slanders so her judgment

speaker: CLOTEN
text: That what’s else rare is choked. And in that point

speaker: CLOTEN
text: I will conclude to hate her, nay, indeed,

speaker: CLOTEN
text: To be revenged upon her. For, when fools

speaker: CLOTEN
text: Shall—

speaker: CLOTEN
text: Who is here? What, are you packing, sirrah?

speaker: CLOTEN
text: Come hither. Ah, you precious pander! Villain,

speaker: CLOTEN
text: Where is thy lady? In a word, or else

speaker: CLOTEN
text: Thou art straightway with the fiends.

speaker: PISANIO
text: O, good my lord—

speaker: CLOTEN
text: Where is thy lady? Or, by Jupiter—

speaker: CLOTEN
text: I will not ask again. Close villain,

speaker: CLOTEN
text: I’ll have this secret from thy heart or rip

speaker: CLOTEN
text: Thy heart to find it. Is she with Posthumus,

speaker: CLOTEN
text: From whose so many weights of baseness cannot

speaker: CLOTEN
text: A dram of worth be drawn?

speaker: PISANIO
text: Alas, my lord,

speaker: PISANIO
text: How can she be with him? When was she missed?

speaker: PISANIO
text: He is in Rome.

speaker: CLOTEN
text: Where is she, sir? Come nearer.

speaker: CLOTEN
text: No farther halting. Satisfy me home

speaker: CLOTEN
text: What is become of her.

speaker: PISANIO
text: O, my all-worthy lord!

speaker: CLOTEN
text: All-worthy villain!

speaker: CLOTEN
text: Discover where thy mistress is at once,

speaker: CLOTEN
text: At the next word. No more of “worthy lord”!

speaker: CLOTEN
text: Speak, or thy silence on the instant is

speaker: CLOTEN
text: Thy condemnation and thy death.

speaker: PISANIO
text: Then, sir,

speaker: PISANIO
text: This paper is the history of my knowledge

speaker: PISANIO
text: Touching her flight.

speaker: CLOTEN
text: Let’s see ’t. I will pursue her

speaker: CLOTEN
text: Even to Augustus’ throne.

speaker: PISANIO
text: Or this or perish.

speaker: PISANIO
text: She’s far enough, and what he learns by this

speaker: PISANIO
text: May prove his travail, not her danger.

speaker: CLOTEN
text: Humh!

speaker: PISANIO
text: I’ll write to my lord she’s dead. O Imogen,

speaker: PISANIO
text: Safe mayst thou wander, safe return again!

speaker: CLOTEN
text: Sirrah, is this letter true?

speaker: PISANIO
text: Sir, as I think.

speaker: CLOTEN
text: It is Posthumus’ hand, I know ’t. Sirrah, if

speaker: CLOTEN
text: thou wouldst not be a villain, but do me true service,

speaker: CLOTEN
text: undergo those employments wherein I should

speaker: CLOTEN
text: have cause to use thee with a serious industry—

speaker: CLOTEN
text: that is, what villainy soe’er I bid thee do to perform

speaker: CLOTEN
text: it directly and truly—I would think thee an honest

speaker: CLOTEN
text: man. Thou shouldst neither want my means for thy

speaker: CLOTEN
text: relief nor my voice for thy preferment.

speaker: PISANIO
text: Well, my good lord.

speaker: CLOTEN
text: Wilt thou serve me? For since patiently and

speaker: CLOTEN
text: constantly thou hast stuck to the bare fortune of

speaker: CLOTEN
text: that beggar Posthumus, thou canst not in the

speaker: CLOTEN
text: course of gratitude but be a diligent follower of

speaker: CLOTEN
text: mine. Wilt thou serve me?

speaker: PISANIO
text: Sir, I will.

speaker: CLOTEN
text: Give me thy hand. Here’s my purse.

speaker: CLOTEN
text: Hast any of thy late master’s garments

speaker: CLOTEN
text: in thy possession?

speaker: PISANIO
text: I have, my lord, at my lodging the same suit he

speaker: PISANIO
text: wore when he took leave of my lady and mistress.

speaker: CLOTEN
text: The first service thou dost me, fetch that suit

speaker: CLOTEN
text: hither. Let it be thy first service. Go.

speaker: PISANIO
text: I shall, my lord.

speaker: CLOTEN
text: Meet thee at Milford Haven!—I forgot to ask

speaker: CLOTEN
text: him one thing; I’ll remember ’t anon. Even there,

speaker: CLOTEN
text: thou villain Posthumus, will I kill thee. I would

speaker: CLOTEN
text: these garments were come. She said upon a time—

speaker: CLOTEN
text: the bitterness of it I now belch from my heart—

speaker: CLOTEN
text: that she held the very garment of Posthumus in

speaker: CLOTEN
text: more respect than my noble and natural person,

speaker: CLOTEN
text: together with the adornment of my qualities. With

speaker: CLOTEN
text: that suit upon my back will I ravish her. First, kill

speaker: CLOTEN
text: him, and in her eyes. There shall she see my valor,

speaker: CLOTEN
text: which will then be a torment to her contempt.

speaker: CLOTEN
text: He on the ground, my speech of insultment

speaker: CLOTEN
text: ended on his dead body, and when my lust hath

speaker: CLOTEN
text: dined—which, as I say, to vex her I will execute

speaker: CLOTEN
text: in the clothes that she so praised—to the court

speaker: CLOTEN
text: I’ll knock her back, foot her home again. She hath

speaker: CLOTEN
text: despised me rejoicingly, and I’ll be merry in my

speaker: CLOTEN
text: revenge.

speaker: CLOTEN
text: Be those the garments?

speaker: PISANIO
text: Ay, my noble lord.

speaker: CLOTEN
text: How long is ’t since she went to Milford Haven?

speaker: PISANIO
text: She can scarce be there yet.

speaker: CLOTEN
text: Bring this apparel to my chamber; that is the

speaker: CLOTEN
text: second thing that I have commanded thee. The

speaker: CLOTEN
text: third is that thou wilt be a voluntary mute to my

speaker: CLOTEN
text: design. Be but duteous, and true preferment shall

speaker: CLOTEN
text: tender itself to thee. My revenge is now at Milford.

speaker: CLOTEN
text: Would I had wings to follow it! Come, and be true.

speaker: PISANIO
text: Thou bidd’st me to my loss, for true to thee

speaker: PISANIO
text: Were to prove false, which I will never be,

speaker: PISANIO
text: To him that is most true. To Milford go,

speaker: PISANIO
text: And find not her whom thou pursuest. Flow, flow,

speaker: PISANIO
text: You heavenly blessings, on her. This fool’s speed

speaker: PISANIO
text: Be crossed with slowness. Labor be his meed.
